title_original: Quantum Architecture Search with Unsupervised Representation Learning – Quantum
summary_original: Researchers Yize Sun, Zixin Wu, Volker Tresp, and Yunpu Ma from Ludwig-Maximilians-University Munich, Siemens AG, and MCML introduce an unsupervised machine learning framework to improve quantum circuit design on Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum devices. Their approach, detailed in Quantum (volume 10, page 1994, 2026), decouples representation learning from circuit search, using graph-based encodings and reinforcement or Bayesian optimization to efficiently explore circuit space without labeled performance data. Experimental validation includes running optimized MaxCut circuits on IBM’s ibm_sherbrooke processor, where performance was retained despite hardware noise. This work demonstrates that unsupervised representation learning streamlines the search for high-performing quantum circuits, requiring significantly fewer evaluations and enabling scalable quantum architecture design for current quantum hardware.
